type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: CO stack tests in 2015 indicated that CO emission levels were significantly lower than in 2014

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: 2013 NOx emissions were abnormally high in the first quarter of 2013.  It is unclear if this was a result of combustion tuning issues that continued after the 2012 upgrade. Subsequent quarters have been more consistent.

comments.comment: The fuel composition was different than previous years

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The stack-test based emission factor used has decreased and is now consistent with previous emission tests. The 2013 factor was higher as a result of an anomolous high test result.  Recent testing suggests emission rates are more consistent with previous observed testing at the facility.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: A review of AP-42 literature suggests that almost all particulate matter from natural gas combustion is of a size fraction that all particulate will meet the definition of PM10.  Therefore it is assumed that all particulate emitted based on stack testing meets the definition of PM10. As outlined in the TPM commentary recent testing suggests all particulate emissions are reduced inline with previous testing (2013 was an anomolous high year)

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The stack-test based emission factor uses has increased due to elevated particulate levels in one test which occurred just the plants 2012 upgrade. type.en: Reason why substance was added to the report
type.fr: Raison pour laquelle la substance a été ajoutée au rapport

comments.comment: A review of AP-42 literature suggests that almost all particulate matter from natural gas combustion is of a size fraction that all particulate will meet the definition of PM2.5 and therefore also meet the definition of PM10.  Therefore, for completeness of the submission it was assumed that all particulate releases, based on total particulate stack testing, would also equal the PM10 and PM2.5 releases for the facility.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
COVID-19 response and recovery aspects resulted in a reduced demand for electricity in New Brunswick during 2020. Operational requirements, market conditions and price influenced when fossil unit operation was required during the year. Gross generation for Bayside increased 201.22 percent from 315,250.7 MWhr in 2019 to 634,354.2 MWHr in 2020 

Additionally, gross generation for NB Power’s coal-fired Belledune Generating Station decreased 36.9 percent from 2.9151 TWHr in 2019 to 1.8397 TWHr in 2020; as a result of a lower capacity factor for Belledune in 2020. Gross generation for NB Power’s heavy fuel oil-fired Coleson Cove Generating Station decreased 62.2 percent from 319,602 MWh in 2019 to 120,816 MWh in 2020 during the year as a result of a lower capacity factor for the station.

Other Factors Contributing to Performance

• Emission rates vary due to several factors including fuel utilization and blend, boiler condition, operating load, and hours of operation. The availability of hydro and nuclear generation influences the capacity factor of fossil-fired units on an annual basis.
• Wind generation is a valuable resource as it helps NB Power meet the 40 per cent renewable portfolio standard. Wind is also an intermittent resource, with varied and largely unpredictable generation presenting challenges of balancing intermittent resources such as wind or solar. Additionally, any thermal units needed to meet the next day’s peak demand, balance load or balance renewables must remain online, as these units cannot be cycled on and off daily.
• The emission intensity rates vary during operation of the fossil-fired generating stations. During times of electricity purchases and / or decreased energy demand, lower operating loads can result in less efficient generation from fossil-fired stations. This is a function of the physical nature of the boilers and the thermodynamics under which they operate.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment:
Gross generation for Bayside increased 132.3 percent from 634,354.2 MWHr in 2020 to 839,044 MWHr in 2021 as a result of a higher capacity factor in 2021. 

Factors Contributing to Annual Emissions in 2021

NB Power continued its journey along the path towards a post-pandemic recovery. Gradual improvements in provincial conditions reflected economic conditions similar to pre-pandemic levels. Higher generation requirements, to meet increased sales volumes, were experienced during the year. Operational requirements, market conditions and price influenced when fossil unit operation was required during the year. Overall, NB Power fossil-fuel generation increased 31.7%. 

Emission rates vary due to several factors including fuel utilization and blend, boiler condition, operating load, and hours of operation. The availability of hydro and nuclear generation influences the capacity factor of fossil-fired units on an annual basis.

The percentage of in-province sales, for calendar year 2021, supplied by renewable and non-emitting resources was approximately 79%; above the five year average (2016-2020) of 75.6 %. In determining this percentage, NB Power includes generation and purchases; renewable and non-emitting includes NB Power’s own Hydro and Nuclear generation as well as purchases from renewable and non-emitting sources (hydro, wind, biomass and other embedded generation).

Renewal generation (wind and solar) is an intermittent resource, with varied and largely unpredictable generation. This variability creates an average daily ramping requirement. This highlights the challenges of balancing intermittent resources such as wind or solar. Additionally, any thermal units needed to meet the next day’s peak demand, balance load or balance renewables must remain online, as these units cannot be cycled on and off on a daily basis.

The emission intensity rates vary during operation of the fossil-fired generating stations. During times of electricity purchases and / or decreased energy demand, lower operating loads can result in less efficient generation from fossil-fired stations. This is a function of the physical nature of the boilers and the thermodynamics under which they operate.

This highlights the challenges of balancing intermittent resources such as wind or solar. Additionally, any thermal units needed to meet the next day’s peak demand, balance load or balance renewables must remain online, as these units cannot be cycled on and off on a daily basis.

The emission intensity rates vary during operation of the fossil-fired generating stations. During times of electricity purchases and / or decreased energy demand, lower operating loads can result in less efficient generation from fossil-fired stations. This is a function of the physical nature of the boilers and the thermodynamics under which they operate.
